questions.question: Rust is a memory-safe language without a garbage collector.
questions.explanation: Rust achieves memory safety through its ownership system and compile-time checks.

questions.question: The 'Option' enum is used to handle values that might be missing.
questions.explanation: It has two variants: Some(T) and None.

questions.question: A 'Trait' defines shared behavior across different types.
questions.explanation: Traits are similar to interfaces in other languages.

questions.question: By default, all variables in Rust can be mutated.
questions.explanation: Variables are immutable by default; you must use the 'mut' keyword to make them mutable.

questions.question: A 'Reference' allows you to borrow a value without taking ownership.
questions.explanation: References are created using the '&' symbol.

questions.question: The 'unwrap()' method on a Result will cause a panic if it contains an Error.
questions.explanation: Using unwrap is a common way to quickly handle results in prototypes but should be avoided in robust code.
